rationale: The page contains a historical map of South Asia in 1475 CE, illustrating the political boundaries of various empires, sultanates, and regional powers such as the Delhi Sultanate (Lodis), Vijayanagara Empire, and Bahmani Sultanate. This provides geographical context for late 15th-century Indian history.
cleanedText:
South-Asia 1475 CE
Historical Map Labels:
- Timurid Empire
- Shah Mir Sultanate
- Phagmodrupas
- Delhi Sultanate (Lodis)
- Sharqis (Cities indicated: Badayun, Kalpi, Jaunpur)
- Sugaunas
- Chutia
- Ahom
- Kamatas
- Tripwa
- Bengal Sultanate
- Gondwana
- Eastern Gangas
- Bahmani Sultanate
- Vijayanagara Empire
- Khandesh Sultanate
- Malwa Sultanate
- Gujarat Sultanate
- Rajputana
- Tomaras
- Sammas
- Kalmat
